question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect node records to be exported by querying when the node table has multiple rows for the same node_id (e.g., due to a data ingestion bug), causing the node_info CTE to return multiple rows per node and the JOIN to produce duplicate or incorrect node records in the export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records in the export by querying with a minNodeId of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the abe.node_id >= :minNodeId condition to exclude all nodes and return an empty result, even though valid nodes exist in the address book?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying with a maxNodeId of 0, causing the abe.node_id <= :maxNodeId condition to exclude all nodes with node_id > 0 and return only nodes with node_id = 0, which may not represent the actual network membership?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying with a nodeIds array containing a single element that matches a node not in the latest address book, causing the query to return an empty result even though the node exists in an older address book, misleading consumers about node availability?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect stake_not_rewarded values to be exported by querying nodes where stake_not_rewarded is exactly -1 in the database, causing NULLIF(ns.stake_not_rewarded, -1) to return NULL and the exported record to omit this field, even if -1 is a meaningful value in the staking protocol?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book table has no rows for the requested fileId, causing the latest_address_book CTE to return zero rows and the main query to produce an empty result, with no error surfaced to indicate the fileId is invalid?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_service_endpoint table has rows with NULL ip_address_v4 and NULL domain_name, causing the COALESCE expressions to substitute empty strings and produce misleading endpoint objects in the serviceEndpointsJson field of the exported record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_service_endpoint table has rows with NULL port, causing the jsonb_build_object to include a null port value in the serviceEndpointsJson, which may be misinterpreted by consumers expecting a valid port number?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node_cert_hash field contains a value that already starts with '0x' in its UTF-8 representation, causing the CASE expression to return it as-is, but if the value is double-prefixed (e.g., '0x0x...'), the exported record contains a malformed hash?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the public_key field already starts with '0x', causing the CASE expression to return it as-is, but if the stored value is malformed (e.g., '0x' with no actual key bytes), the exported record contains a misleading non-empty publicKey?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node_stake table has a staking_period of NULL for the latest consensus_timestamp, causing the mapStakingPeriod() method in NetworkNodeMapper to return null and omit the staking period from the exported record, even though a staking period should exist?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect staking period timestamps to be exported by querying when the stakingPeriod value in the node_stake table is near Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the mapStakingPeriod() calculation (stakingPeriod + 1L) to overflow and produce a negative or incorrect timestamp in the exported TimestampRangeNullable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect staking period end timestamps to be exported by querying when the staking period calculation (from + 86400L * DomainUtils.NANOS_PER_SECOND) overflows a Long, producing a negative or incorrect end timestamp in the exported TimestampRangeNullable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect timestamp range data to be exported by querying when both startConsensusTimestamp and endConsensusTimestamp are NULL for a node, causing mapTimestampRange() to return null and the exported NetworkNode to have no timestamp field, even though a timestamp range should always be present?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the serviceEndpointsJson field contains malformed JSON (e.g., due to a database corruption or encoding issue), causing parseServiceEndpointList() in NetworkNodeMapper to throw a RuntimeException and crash the export pipeline instead of returning a partial or empty result?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the grpcProxyEndpointJson field contains malformed JSON, causing parseServiceEndpoint() in NetworkNodeMapper to throw a RuntimeException and abort the entire export response rather than returning the node with a null grpcProxyEndpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node_info CTE selects a node row that has been soft-deleted or marked inactive, since the query SELECT account_id, admin_key, associated_registered_nodes, decline_reward, grpc_proxy_endpoint, node_id FROM node has no filter for active/deleted status, causing stale node metadata to appear in the export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_entry table has multiple rows for the same node_id at the same consensus_timestamp (data integrity violation), causing the JOIN to produce duplicate node records in the export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records in the export by querying with a nodeIds array that is non-empty but contains only node IDs that do not exist in the latest address book, causing the query to return an empty result with no indication that the requested nodes are absent from the current network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node_stake table has a reward_rate of NULL for the latest consensus_timestamp, causing the rewardRateStart field in the exported record to be NULL and consumers to incorrectly assume no reward rate is configured?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node_stake table has a stake_rewarded of NULL, causing the stakeRewarded field in the exported record to be NULL and consumers to misinterpret the staking state of the node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node table has a grpc_proxy_endpoint JSONB field with an unexpected schema (e.g., extra or missing keys), causing the jsonb_build_object construction to produce an incomplete or incorrect endpoint object in the exported record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_entry table has a node_cert_hash that is valid bytea but whose UTF-8 conversion produces a string that does not start with '0x', causing the ELSE branch to prepend '0x' and create a hash value that does not match the original certificate hash?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_entry table has a description or memo field containing special characters or SQL-unsafe content that is returned as-is in the exported record, potentially causing downstream consumers to misparse the exported data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the node_stake table has a stake value of 0, causing the exported stake field to be 0 (not NULL, since NULLIF only checks for -1), and consumers to incorrectly interpret a zero stake as a valid active staking amount?
